profile.name: Kaung Myat Tun
profile.role: Embedded Electronics | PCB Hardware | Intelligent Sensing Systems
profile.summary: Electronics and embedded systems engineer focused on intelligent sensing, mixed-signal PCB hardware, firmware, robotics systems, and practical hardware/software integration.
profile.why: PLACEHOLDER - EDIT ME: I like hardware because it doesn't let you hide from a bad decision - the scope trace either matches the math or it doesn't.
profile.nextFocus: PLACEHOLDER - EDIT ME: Next, I'm aiming toward low-power sensor IC and mixed-signal roles, building on the tapeout and PCB work above.

skills.summary: CMOS circuit design, SPICE simulation, open-source PDK flow, and layout verification.
RTL design, FPGA implementation, bus interfaces, pipelining, and clock-domain crossing.
Simulation-first hardware development with testbenches, formal checks, CI, and coverage.
Mixed-signal board design, sensor interfaces, ADC signal chains, isolation, and practical bring-up.
Firmware, C/C++, RTOS concepts, board-level integration, and low-level debugging.
Python-based analysis, automation, early ML work, and data-structure/software projects.
Bench-level debugging and measurement workflows for electronics projects.

projects.title: Axon-Hillock spiking neuron
projects.summary: Analog tapeout project implementing an Axon-Hillock spiking neuron model on the Sky130 open-source PDK.
projects.details: Schematic capture and SPICE simulation are complete; full-custom layout is underway in Magic with LVS/DRC verification through Netgen. The project is framed as a silicon credential and a step toward ultra-low-power sensor IC research.
projects.imageAlt: Oscilloscope-style trace for an analog spiking neuron project

projects.title: VibeCheck dual-channel OTA and FIR DSP
projects.summary: Mixed-signal tapeout combining a two-stage Miller-compensated OTA with a dual-channel FIR DSP block.
projects.details: This project demonstrates analog front-end design paired with digital signal processing on the same die, making it a strong portfolio centerpiece for mixed-signal IC roles.

projects.title: AXI4-Lite FIR filter accelerator
projects.summary: Standalone digital design project with AXI4-Lite control, FIR datapath logic, CDC handling, and verification.
projects.details: Built to demonstrate RTL architecture and verification methodology independent of analog tapeout work. Test coverage includes cocotb simulation and clock-domain-crossing checks.

projects.title: CDC-safe asynchronous FIFO
projects.summary: Asynchronous FIFO using Gray-coded pointers for safe clock-domain crossing.
projects.details: Verified with cocotb-driven simulation and formal checks for CDC hazards using SymbiYosys. Prepared around interview topics including metastability, CDC, and Gray coding.

projects.title: Pipelined AES-128 FPGA implementation
projects.summary: Fully pipelined AES-128 implementation with CI-gated linting, simulation, and coverage.
projects.details: Treats an FPGA project with production-style rigor by running Verible linting, Verilator/cocotb simulation, and branch coverage reporting on every push.

projects.title: Dual ADS8568 FMC carrier board
projects.summary: Custom PCB carrying dual ADS8568 SAR ADCs in parallel 16-bit mode over an FMC interface.
projects.details: Involved full signal-chain analysis, component selection, and Altium PCB design for an FPGA-connected data acquisition board.
projects.imageAlt: ADC FMC carrier board project preview

projects.title: Three-phase motor current-sensing board
projects.stack: LEM LA55-P, OPA2197, ISO224, LTspice, Altium
projects.summary: Isolated signal-conditioning board for three-phase motor current sensing.
projects.details: Uses LEM LA55-P Hall-effect sensors, OPA2197 op-amps, ISO224 isolation amplifiers, REF6025 reference, and isolated DC-DC conversion. Simulated in LTspice and laid out in Altium.
projects.imageAlt: Motor current sensing board project preview

projects.title: Embedded firmware and RISC-V systems
projects.stack: STM32, Zephyr RTOS, nRF52840, RISC-V RTL
projects.summary: Grouped embedded-systems work covering firmware, RTOS development, and processor architecture.
projects.details: Shows the firmware and computer-architecture layer behind the mixed-signal and FPGA projects, including bare-metal STM32 work, Zephyr RTOS development, and RISC-V pipeline RTL.

experience.title: Air Products
experience.summary: Worked on industrial IIoT sensing systems across firmware, custom PCB prototypes, wireless devices, and live data infrastructure.
experience.details: The role connected embedded hardware with real industrial monitoring workflows: device firmware, wireless modules, 4-layer prototype PCBs, sensor data pipelines, and dashboarding for real-time engineering visibility.
highlights: Developed C and C# firmware for Nordic, Silicon Labs, and U-blox chipsets.
Prototyped custom 4-layer IIoT PCBs and supported hardware bring-up/debug.
Contributed to Python, Docker, MQTT, machine-learning, and Grafana data pipelines.

experience.title: Project MarsWorks
experience.summary: Developed rover localisation, sensor integration, motor control, and robotics software for a student Mars rover platform.
experience.details: This experience demonstrates practical robotics engineering: turning sensor inputs into navigation behaviour, integrating software with physical hardware, and testing mobility systems under realistic constraints.
highlights: Built localisation and SLAM workflows using ROS and computer vision.
Integrated IMU and encoder inputs for motion estimation.
Designed and tested motor control systems for stable rover movement.
experience.imageAlt: Mars rover software and electronics project

experience.title: Pipebots, University of Sheffield
experience.summary: Built and tested visual navigation software for autonomous robot motion estimation using optical-flow methods.
experience.details: The work focused on using live camera data to estimate movement and position, then validating the navigation approach across different surfaces to understand reliability in real-world conditions.
highlights: Used the RAFT optical-flow algorithm for live visual motion estimation.
Processed camera data in real time to estimate robot movement and position.
Validated performance across multiple surface types.

education.title: University of Sheffield
education.tag: MEng Electrical and Electronic Engineering with an Industrial Placement Year
education.summary: A one-year industrial placement was integrated into the course.
